name: Agent Cloud
description: Agent Cloud is an open-source platform by RNA Digital for building RAG-first, data-connected AI agents. Core differentiator is datasource-first architecture: connect databases, cloud storage, APIs, web scrapers, then build agents grounded in that data. Qdrant vector DB, CrewAI multi-agent support, Airbyte for 300+ connectors.
oneLiner: Open-source platform for building RAG-first, data-connected AI agents with visual web UI and multi-datasource pipelines.
funding: Bootstrapped by RNA Digital (Sydney-based consultancy). No announced VC rounds.
pricing: Open Source (AGPL v3, free self-hosted); Cloud SaaS with free/paid tiers; Enterprise custom
capabilities: Multi-datasource connectivity (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, S3, GCS)

uxAnalysis.builderFlow: Web-based admin UI: (1) Connect datasources — select type, configure connection, set embedding/chunking. (2) Create agent — name, model, prompt, attach datasources/tools. (3) Optionally create crew with multiple agents. (4) Chat via built-in interface. Functional but admin-panel aesthetic.
strengths: Data-first: datasource config is first-class, not afterthought
Low RAG barrier: connect data and chat in minutes without code
Docker Compose self-hosting is genuinely simple
weaknesses: AGPL v3 may deter some enterprises
notableFeatures: Datasource-first architecture with visual connection wizard
CrewAI multi-agent crews via web UI
Multi-provider LLM support including Ollama local models

recentDevelopments: 2026: Agentic RAG market evolving — agents now plan, retrieve, reason, critique in loops rather than fixed sequences
2026: Industry shift toward contextual memory (agentic memory) over basic RAG for operational AI deployments
2025: Continued active development on agent and datasource features
2025: Added LLM providers and embedding models

deploymentOptions.notes: Docker-based, can self-host. Also offers hosted version.

subCriteria.name: Agent Builder UX
subCriteria.rationale: Web UI guides datasource connection, agent config, deployment. Functional, not polished.

subCriteria.name: Time-to-Value
subCriteria.rationale: Chat testing and session history. No trace visualization or testing framework.

subCriteria.name: Debugging & Testing
subCriteria.rationale: README and docs cover setup. Docker Compose makes self-hosting easy. Gaps on production.

subCriteria.name: Documentation & Learning
subCriteria.rationale: Config changes apply immediately. No version control or rollback.

subCriteria.name: Multi-Agent Coordination
subCriteria.rationale: CrewAI integration for multi-agent crews. Not native orchestration.

subCriteria.name: Human-in-the-Loop Design
subCriteria.rationale: Chat interaction only. No structured handoff or evidence packets.

subCriteria.name: Workflow Templates
subCriteria.rationale: Basic org/team model. No Git integration or version control.

subCriteria.name: Team Development
subCriteria.rationale: No pre-built templates. Configure from scratch.

subCriteria.name: Native Connectors
subCriteria.rationale: Standout: native DB/file/web connectors + Airbyte for 300+ sources. Above average for OSS.

subCriteria.name: API & Protocol Standards
subCriteria.rationale: REST API for platform ops. No MCP or standardized tool protocols.

subCriteria.name: Authentication & Access
subCriteria.rationale: Basic OAuth for datasources. No SAML or per-agent scoping.

subCriteria.name: Data Pipeline & RAG
subCriteria.rationale: Standout. Full RAG pipeline: connect -> load -> chunk -> embed -> retrieve. Configurable.

subCriteria.name: Guardrails & Kill Switches
subCriteria.rationale: No kill switches, guardrails, or behavioral boundaries.

subCriteria.name: Audit Trail & Compliance
subCriteria.rationale: Chat provides implicit oversight. No configurable approval workflows.

subCriteria.name: RBAC & Access Control
subCriteria.rationale: Conversation history stored. No immutable logs or compliance certs.

subCriteria.name: Explainability & Transparency
subCriteria.rationale: Basic team model. No RBAC or per-resource access.

subCriteria.name: Deployment Flexibility
subCriteria.rationale: Docker Compose self-hosted. Cloud SaaS. No K8s operator or air-gapped support.

subCriteria.name: Scaling & Monitoring
subCriteria.rationale: Single-instance by default. No horizontal scaling or auto-scaling built in.

subCriteria.name: Cost Management
subCriteria.rationale: Basic app logs. No monitoring dashboards or alerting.

subCriteria.name: Ecosystem Maturity
subCriteria.rationale: ~1.2K GitHub stars. Small growing community. Limited coverage.

scoringRationale: 46/100 overall. Strongest: Integration (12/20). Weakest: Governance (6/20). Breakdown: Creation (11/20), Orchestration (8/20), Integration (12/20), Governance (6/20), Operations (9/20).
targetUser: Teams building data-connected agents with enterprise data sources

walkthrough.title: Building a Data Extraction Pipeline with Agent Cloud

steps.title: Connect Enterprise Data Sources
steps.description: Link your databases, APIs, and document stores to Agent Cloud. Configure connectors for PostgreSQL, BigQuery, S3, or custom APIs. The platform handles authentication, schema discovery, and connection pooling.

works: Clean management UI for creating and monitoring agents
Multi-LLM support lets teams experiment with different providers
Docker-based self-hosting gives full control over data
doesntWork: UI is functional but lacks polish — feels like an admin panel, not a product
Agent debugging tools are basic — limited to log inspection
No visual workflow builder — text-based configuration only
pmCritique.userPsychology: Self-hosted agent management fills a real gap — teams want control without building from scratch. But the admin-panel aesthetic signals early-stage, which makes enterprise buyers hesitant to depend on it.

steps.title: Configure RAG with Embedding Models
steps.description: Select your embedding model and chunking strategy. Agent Cloud vectorizes your documents and stores them in its built-in vector database, creating a searchable knowledge layer your agents can query in real-time.

works: Agent creation wizard walks through required fields step by step
Tool integration uses standard function calling patterns
Conversation testing interface for interactive debugging
doesntWork: Wizard does not validate configuration until the end — errors are caught late
Tool ecosystem is limited — must build custom integrations
No agent versioning — changes are destructive
pmCritique.userPsychology: The wizard reduces initial friction but the lack of validation creates a frustrating try-fail-try loop. Developers want fast feedback — knowing something is wrong immediately, not after clicking through 5 steps.

steps.title: Build Extraction Agents in the Web UI
steps.description: Define agents with specific extraction goals — pull financial metrics from earnings reports, extract product specs from catalogs, or summarize customer feedback. Assign tools, models, and output schemas to each agent.

steps.title: Orchestrate Multi-Agent Crews
steps.description: Compose agents into crews using CrewAI integration. A research agent finds sources, an extraction agent pulls structured data, and a validation agent checks accuracy. Run pipelines on schedule or via webhook triggers.

steps.title: Deploy Self-Hosted or Cloud
steps.description: Run your extraction pipeline on Agent Cloud's managed infrastructure or deploy self-hosted with Docker. Monitor agent performance, review extracted data quality, and iterate on agent instructions.

pmVerdict.summary: Agent Cloud — limited public demo content available. Verdict based on documentation and existing research data only. Score reflects capability claims that could not be verified through video walkthrough.
strengths: See existing capability analysis in the product profile
risks: No video demo available for verification
Product capabilities based on documentation only
UX quality unverifiable without live demo

ctoVerdict.summary: Insufficient public demo material to evaluate Agent Cloud for enterprise adoption. Recommend requesting a live demo from the vendor before making any commitment.
strengths: See existing scoring for capability assessment
dealBreakers: Cannot verify UX claims without walkthrough

youtubeReviews.title: Agent Cloud Introduction + RAG Google Bigquery!

coreCompetence.bestAt: Open-source agent management platform with a clean UI for building, testing, and deploying conversational AI agents backed by any LLM.
coreCompetence.targetUser: Small development teams who want a self-hosted agent management UI without vendor lock-in. Teams exploring multiple LLM providers.
coreCompetence.vsAlternatives: Wins on self-hosting flexibility and clean management UI. Open-source transparency. Loses on maturity and scale vs LangGraph Platform. Loses on integration depth vs enterprise platforms.
